description_long: This object is a compact, historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four small wheels. The cannon barrel is metallic, cylindrical, and features reinforcing bands and a flared muzzle, suggesting it is designed for firing projectiles. The carriage is constructed from wood, with visible bolts and metal fittings securing the cannon in place. The wheels are small and robust, suitable for limited movement across flat surfaces. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible modern features such as electronics or armor plating. The asset sits on a flat, grassy terrain, emphasizing its use as a ground-based artillery piece.
description: A compact, historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden wheeled carriage, featuring a metallic barrel and robust fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the metallic cannon barrel and the wooden carriage with four wheels.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the carriage wheels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the cannon barrel and carriage from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage and the breech end of the cannon barrel.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the object is painted in solid brown and metallic tones.
